title: Beyond Good and Evil - Herd Morality
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use as diagnosis of herd conformity and moralized comfort, not as contempt for ordinary people.
misreading_risk: Can be misread as blanket hostility to community or kindness.

title: Beyond Good and Evil - Master and Slave Morality
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain contrasting value systems, not to glorify domination or degrade vulnerable people.
misreading_risk: Can be distorted into crude strong-versus-weak ideology.

title: Beyond Good and Evil - Rank and the Pathos of Distance
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to discuss self-command, standards, and differentiation, not social contempt or class superiority.
misreading_risk: Can be misread as endorsement of elitist cruelty.

title: Beyond Good and Evil - Suffering and Self-Overcoming
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain that hardship can be formative, not that suffering is good in itself.
misreading_risk: Can be oversimplified into macho pain-worship.

title: The Gay Science - Against Moral Self-Satisfaction
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to question automatic moral certainty and inherited conscience.
misreading_risk: Can be read as denying all ethical seriousness.

title: The Gay Science - Amor Fati
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to frame affirmation of reality without romanticizing suffering or passivity.
misreading_risk: Can be flattened into vague positivity or fatalism.

title: The Gay Science - Beyond the Categorical Imperative
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain Nietzsche’s resistance to one-size-fits-all morality.
misreading_risk: Can be mistaken for permission to do whatever one wants.

title: The Gay Science - Give Style to One's Character
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to discuss shaping the self through form and discipline, not surface image management.
misreading_risk: Can be reduced to branding or aesthetic vanity.

title: The Gay Science - Pioneers
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to encourage risk in thought and growth, not recklessness for its own sake.
misreading_risk: Can be turned into empty thrill-seeking rhetoric.

title: On the Genealogy of Morality - Ascetic Ideal and the Meaning of Suffering
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain how suffering seeks meaning and how nihilistic meanings can take hold.
misreading_risk: Can be misread as mere contempt for religion or for suffering people.

title: On the Genealogy of Morality - The Ascetic Priest
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain management of suffering through guilt and redirected resentment.
misreading_risk: Can be overextended into simplistic attacks on all care or all religion.

title: On the Genealogy of Morality - Bad Conscience and Internalization
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain self-attack and inwardly turned force, not to dismiss all conscience.
misreading_risk: Can be oversimplified into anti-responsibility slogans.

title: On the Genealogy of Morality - Birds of Prey and Lambs
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use as analysis of moralized weakness and reactive rediagnosis, not as praise of brutality.
misreading_risk: Can be used to justify predatory behavior.

title: On the Genealogy of Morality - Debt, Guilt, and God
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain moralized debt and guilt, not to trivialize real harm or accountability.
misreading_risk: Can be misread as reducing all morality to economics.

title: On the Genealogy of Morality - Noble Valuation and the Origin of Good
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain the historical contrast between noble and moralized valuation.
misreading_risk: Can be read as mere aristocratic nostalgia.

title: On the Genealogy of Morality - Ressentiment and the Slave Revolt
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain reactive bitterness and value inversion, not as a lazy insult toward the hurt or disadvantaged.
misreading_risk: Can be weaponized as a dismissive label.

title: Guide - Herd Morality Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to translate herd morality into modern psychological and social terms.
misreading_risk: Can drift into shallow anti-social posturing if used crudely.

title: Guide - Independence and Rank Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain inward standards, self-command, and differentiation without endorsing elitist contempt.
misreading_risk: Can be misread as social superiority doctrine.

title: Guide - Amor Fati Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain affirmative relation to reality without collapsing into passivity or spiritual fluff.
misreading_risk: Can become vague positivity or passive resignation.

title: Guide - Give Style to Character Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to translate self-shaping into practical language about discipline, form, and arrangement.
misreading_risk: Can be flattened into image management.

title: Guide - Bad Conscience Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain inwardly turned aggression and chronic self-accusation.
misreading_risk: Can be mistaken for dismissal of all conscience or remorse.

title: Guide - Reactive Morality Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain enemy-centered, reactive living versus genuine value creation.
misreading_risk: Can be reduced to empty anti-moral rhetoric.

title: Guide - Ressentiment Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain morally disguised envy, bitterness, and reactive judgment in modern language.
misreading_risk: Can be misused as a one-word insult.

title: Guide - Nietzsche's Critical Project Overview
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use as a big-picture guide to Nietzsche's critical project.
misreading_risk: Can sound like total rejection of all values if oversimplified.

title: Guide - Free Will, Consciousness, and Responsibility
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain Nietzsche’s challenge to traditional moral agency without collapsing into simplistic determinism.
misreading_risk: Can be taken as denial of all agency.

title: Guide - Genealogy, Master and Slave Morality, and Bad Conscience
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain genealogy as historical-psychological diagnosis.
misreading_risk: Can be reduced to crude strong-good weak-bad slogans.

title: Guide - Higher Types, Autonomy, Self-Creation, and Affirmation
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain Nietzsche’s positive ideal without turning it into vague self-help or social superiority.
misreading_risk: Can be misread as elitist self-mythology.

title: Guide - Nihilism and the Last Man
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain comfort-centered decline and the erosion of higher aims.
misreading_risk: Can be turned into cheap contempt for ordinary life.

title: Guide - Otherworldly Value and the Death of God
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain loss of metaphysical grounding and the afterlife of religious values.
misreading_risk: Can be reduced to edgy atheism.

title: Guide - Pity, Compassion, and Life-Denial
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to clarify Nietzsche’s diagnosis of pity-centered morality without sounding cruel.
misreading_risk: Can be mistaken for anti-kindness.

title: Guide - Nietzsche's Psychological Critique of Morality
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain Nietzsche’s suspicion toward flattering moral self-diagnosis.
misreading_risk: Can devolve into empty cynicism.

title: Guide - Strength Without Self-Deception
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to distinguish real strength from moralized weakness and pose.
misreading_risk: Can drift into macho rhetoric.

title: Guide - Becoming Who You Are Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain self-formation as selection and shaping, not mere self-expression.
misreading_risk: Can become vague authenticity talk.

title: Guide - Creator Versus Herd Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain value-creation and independence from approval.
misreading_risk: Can be misread as simple contrarianism.

title: Guide - The Last Man Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to translate Nietzsche’s diagnosis of smallness and comfort-worship into normal language.
misreading_risk: Can become contempt for ordinary vulnerability.

title: Guide - Self-Overcoming Explained
author: Project
safe_use_note: Use to explain change through confrontation with one’s own inertia and limits.
misreading_risk: Can be reduced to endless self-optimization.

title: Twilight of the Idols - Against Ideal Moralists
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain Nietzsche’s hostility to abstract moral idealizing detached from life.
misreading_risk: Can be mistaken for rejection of every ideal whatsoever.

title: Twilight of the Idols - Beauty, Art, and Power
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to discuss art as enrichment, heightening, and affirmation of life.
misreading_risk: Can be turned into shallow aestheticism.

title: Twilight of the Idols - The Criminal and Greatness Under Pressure
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use carefully as analysis of constrained force and hostile conditions, not as romanticizing criminality.
misreading_risk: Can be read as glamourizing crime.

title: Twilight of the Idols - Goethe
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use as positive model of formed strength, realism, and wholeness.
misreading_risk: Can become vague hero-worship.

title: Twilight of the Idols - Maxims on Strength and Self-Respect
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use for concise Nietzschean challenge around action, self-respect, and courage.
misreading_risk: Can be flattened into motivational one-liners.

title: Twilight of the Idols - Morality as the Enemy of Nature
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain Nietzsche’s diagnosis of anti-instinct moralities and his idea of spiritualizing drives.
misreading_risk: Can be mistaken for indulgence without discipline.

title: Twilight of the Idols - Progress, Freedom, and Modernity
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain Nietzsche’s challenge to modern ideas of freedom and progress.
misreading_risk: Can be misused as blanket anti-modern rant.

title: Twilight of the Idols - The Strong and the Weak
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use very carefully as analysis of flourishing versus decline, not to rank human worth crudely.
misreading_risk: Can be turned into dehumanizing hierarchy talk.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Bestowing Virtue
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain overflowing strength and affirmative giving, not martyrdom or self-erasure.
misreading_risk: Can be confused with conventional altruism.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Companions, Not the Herd
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to distinguish creator-companionship from herd belonging.
misreading_risk: Can be read as contempt for all community.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Eternal Return
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use as test of affirmation and relation to life, not as literal cosmological doctrine for the bot to preach.
misreading_risk: Can confuse users if treated as literal physics.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Last Man
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use as diagnosis of comfort-worship and shrinking affirmation, not as contempt for ordinary limits or recovery.
misreading_risk: Can be read as glorifying cruelty or rejecting all rest.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Old and New Tables
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
section: Old and New Tables, selected excerpt
safe_use_note: Use for value creation, revaluation, and future-oriented self-command.
misreading_risk: Can become empty law-breaking rhetoric.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Preachers of Death
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain life-denying attitudes and moralized weariness, not to mock depression or suffering.
misreading_risk: Can be dangerously misread as contempt for the exhausted.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Self-Surpassing
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to discuss overcoming fixed values and becoming more than one’s current form.
misreading_risk: Can be reduced to domination talk.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Tarantulas
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain revenge disguised as justice or equality, not as blanket rejection of equality in every sense.
misreading_risk: Can be abused for lazy anti-egalitarianism.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Thousand and One Goals
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain created values, plural traditions, and the unfinished question of human goals.
misreading_risk: Can become mushy relativism if flattened.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Three Metamorphoses
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use as symbolic account of transformation from obedience to negation to creation.
misreading_risk: Can be used as empty symbolism without practical meaning.

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Virtuous
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
safe_use_note: Use to explain virtue beyond reward, payback, or moral bookkeeping.
misreading_risk: Can be read as simple rejection of virtue.
